---
title: "Contribute a post"
description: "Share prepared AMMnet Hackathon session materials on the blog."
---

AMMnet Hackathon blog posts turn completed workshops, training sessions, and
other learning materials into resources that the wider community can reuse.
Contributions are welcome from session facilitators, organizers, and
participants working with the session team.

## First, coordinate with the Task Force

Before preparing a post, contact the Hackathon Task Force through the AMMnet
Slack workspace or by email. Share the proposed topic, likely publication
timing, lead contributors, and any expected software, data, or hosting needs.
This helps the Task Force coordinate the schedule and confirm that support and
resources are available.

## Before you begin

Please make sure that:

- the lesson or session materials are complete;
- the authors have agreed to publish them;
- private, sensitive, or copyrighted files have been removed; and
- you know the post title, authors, session date, and a short description.

## Choose a contribution route

### Use an AI assistant

This repository includes a Codex workflow that can turn prepared materials
into a draft post. Open the repository in Codex and ask:

```text
Use $add-ammnet-blog-post to publish the prepared materials at SOURCE.
```

Replace `SOURCE` with the local folder or repository URL containing the
materials. You will review the selected files and finished post before they
are submitted.

### Create the post manually

Follow the [step-by-step contribution guide](https://github.com/AMMnet/ammnet-hackathon/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING_POSTS.md)
to create a Quarto post, preview it locally, and submit a pull request.

## Review and publication

A maintainer will review the pull request and may suggest changes. After it
is approved and merged, the website is rebuilt automatically and the post
appears on the blog. Contributors should check the published page once the
deployment finishes.
